# Persistent Memory MCP

Persistent Memory MCP is an open-source Python Model Context Protocol server for durable software-project memory across AI coding tools.

Documentation: https://dannymaaz.github.io/memory-mcp/
Repository: https://github.com/dannymaaz/memory-mcp
Package: persistent-memory-mcp
Command: memory-mcp
License: MIT
Author: Danny Maaz

## Purpose
It stores project architecture, technical decisions, tasks, warnings, checkpoints, file relationships, timeline events and session state. Compatible MCP clients can retrieve the same project context in later sessions.

## Clients
Codex, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, OpenCode, Qwen Code and other clients with standard MCP server configuration.

## Storage and search
The current backend uses Supabase and PostgreSQL. The included schema enables Row Level Security. Search supports vector similarity when embeddings are supplied and lexical fallback otherwise.

## Installation
pipx install persistent-memory-mcp
memory-mcp init
memory-mcp doctor

Run schema.sql once in Supabase, then register the generated mcpServers configuration in the AI client.

## Relationship to Git
Git stores code history. Persistent Memory MCP stores project reasoning, working state, warnings and next actions that are not fully represented by commits.
